description: §18.8.1 — a match over a MEMBER-ACCESS subject (`match p.role` where role: Role over Role = { Admin, Editor, Viewer }) with only a .Admin arm is non-exhaustive and SHALL fire E-TYPE-020 naming the missing variants. Regression guard: resolveMatchSubjectType previously resolved only bare-identifier headers, never member expressions, so `match p.role` skipped exhaustiveness entirely and compiled clean — silently defeating the §18.8 guarantee and contradicting SPEC's own `match p.role` flagship example at §18.8.1.
rationale: §18.8.1: a match over an enum type SHALL be exhaustive; a non-exhaustive match SHALL be a compile error (E-TYPE-020) listing the missing variant names. A member-access subject resolves to the member's declared field type (Role), so the same exhaustiveness check runs as for a bound-local or ident subject. Missing: Editor, Viewer.
